title: sideritis soluta subsp. soluta
intro: Sideritis soluta subsp. soluta, also known as Greek mountain tea, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Greece and Turkey. It belongs to the mint family and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Sideritis soluta subsp. soluta can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems.
